title_en: RK3588-Camera: MIPI-CSI and ISP Debugging - Path Analysis
text:
Integrating multiple camera sensors into a single system often presents complex data path challenges. Understanding the underlying hardware and software architecture is key to robust image processing.

This post details the MIPI-CSI debugging and ISP path analysis on the RK3588 platform.

⚡ **Multi-sensor support**: Up to 7 sensors (6 MIPI + 1 DVP) can be connected, with a single hardware ISP supporting 4-way multiplexing.
📊 **ISP capabilities**: A single ISP handles resolutions up to 3840x2160 for 2-way multiplexing, or 2560x1536 for 3/4-way.
⚙️ **Hardware PHY configuration**: RK3588 features 2 DPHY hardware units, each configurable in full (4-lane) or split (2x2-lane) modes, alongside 2 DCPHYs supporting DPHY/CPHY protocol multiplexing.
🔄 **VICAP integration**: All camera data routes through a single VICAP unit, which supports simultaneous input from 6 MIPI PHYs and 1 DVP stream, segmented into 7 virtual nodes.
🛡️ **ISP processing modes**: Supports both 'Passthrough' for direct sensor-to-ISP data flow (with HDR nuances) and 'Readback' for DDR-buffered processing, allowing flexible data handling.

This detailed understanding of the RK3588's image pipeline is critical for engineers designing high-performance vision systems, especially in scenarios requiring multiple synchronized camera inputs and advanced image signal processing. Our team at JYTech has been building industrial vision solutions on this architecture through our Sienovo product line.

Full write-up: https://sienovo.jytech.us/blog/131767085
